subject: [PATCH v5 6/6] docs: fuse: document io-uring buffer pool and zero-copy uapi

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation for the Fuse over io-uring usage of buffer pools and zero-copy functionality. It introduces new commands in the uapi (user-space API) to manage buffer pools and enable zero-copy, which requires administrative privileges and a registered buffer pool. The documentation provides details on how to use these features, including setting up buffer pools and enabling zero-copy for optimal performance.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer Amir Goldstein expressed confusion about the naming of io-uring.rst, felt that it was not a suitable standalone name for the document and questioned its unconventional path in Documentation. He also suggested including the details inline in fuse-io-uring.rst instead of having a separate file.
reviews.raw_body:
This dereference is not clear to me.

The name of the suggested doc is even more puzzling for me
io-uring.rst is really not an adequest standalone name for this doc
and fuse/uapi path is unconventional in Documentation.
The doc is not even pure uapi, it explains about buffer pools and zero copy.

Why not include the details inline in fuse-io-uring.rst?
It's not going to be a huge doc and all details seem to be intertwined.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the original documentation location was moved to a separate fuse/uapi directory as per Miklos' preference, and is open to renaming the suggested doc and improving dereference clarity.
reviews.raw_body:
The details were originally in fuse-io-uring.rst but I think Miklos
preferred having a separate fuse/uapi directory [1] for how userspace
interacts with it.

I'm happy to change the name of the suggested doc - is there an
alternative name you'd prefer more? How do you suggest making the
dereference clearer?

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/fuse-devel/CAJfpegt8sfO681aoixfCc2XvUP-OH50GVHG-vmF8Q3oGqxF9MA@mail.gmail.com/#t

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation for using the FUSE file system over io-uring, specifically covering buffer pools and zero-copy functionality. It introduces new commands and flags to enable these features, including adding a queue, registering a buffer pool, and enabling zero-copy. The documentation is split across three files: fuse-io-uring.rst, index.rst, and uapi/io-uring.rst. This patch aims to provide clear guidance for users on how to utilize the FUSE file system with io-uring's advanced features.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Amir Goldstein noted that the documentation should not be moved entirely to the uAPI document, and instead suggested keeping a separate overview of buffer pools in the main fuse-io-uring.rst file.

Reviewer Amir Goldstein suggested reorganizing the documentation by moving commands and structs from fuse-uapi.rst to a separate file, fuse-uapi-io-uring.rst, while keeping the overview of buffer pools and zero-copy in fuse-io-uring.rst.
reviews.raw_body:
My reading of this comment is that we ALSO need a uAPI document
not move the buffer pool overview into the uAPI document.

---

Either document commands and structs in fuse-uapi.rst
(as a placeholder for more commands documentation) or in
fuse-uapi-io-uring.rst.
can be under fuse/uapi/ if this feels more organized.
My point is that doc names can be self describing and I find it better this way.

But I don't think that the overview of buffer pools and zero copy belongs there.
I think they are still very much useful in the fuse-io-uring.rst design doc
as in v4 and I do not think that Miklos objected to this point.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that they misinterpreted the reviewer's feedback and agrees to re-evaluate their understanding of the issue.

Author agreed to restructure the documentation by reviving previously removed paragraphs, indicating a fix is planned for v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Ahhh I see. I reread his comment and yeah you're right, I think I
misinterpreted it.

---

I'll resurrect some of the paragraphs from the previous version and
put it back into the design doc.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation for using the FUSE file system over io-uring, specifically covering buffer pools and zero-copy functionality. It introduces new commands and flags to enable these features, including adding a queue, registering a buffer pool, and enabling zero-copy. The documentation is split across three files: fuse-io-uring.rst, index.rst, and uapi/io-uring.rst. This patch aims to provide clear guidance for users on how to utilize the FUSE file system with io-uring's advanced features.
